sourceMovie.title: Marley & Me
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Marley & Me works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: School of Rock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Marley & Me clicked for you, School of Rock (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Marley & Me?
faq.answer: Try School of Rock, A Dog's Purpose, Meet the Robinsons—each shares DNA with Marley & Me while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Marley & Me?
faq.answer: Marley & Me (2008) is best described as Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Marley & Me part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Marley & Me Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
